title: Update project selection to scope board and files UI only

Input: {
  "subagent_type": "Explore",
  "prompt": "I need to understand how project selection works in this codebase. Specifically:\n\n1. Find where project selection/switching is handled in the UI\n2. Find the app store (Zustand) that manages project state\n3. Understand what happens when a project is selected - what parts of the app get updated\n4. Find the board view and files UI components\n5. Look for any existing patterns of scoped state vs global state\n\nFocus on:\n- apps/ui/src/store/ - app store\n- apps/ui/src/components/views/board-view/ - board view\n- Any project switching/selection components\n- Route handling related to projects\n\nBe thorough - I need to understand the current architecture to plan a change that scopes project selection to only board and files UI.",
  "model": "sonnet",
  "description": "Explore project selection architecture"
}

# Specification: Scoped Board Project Selection

## 1. Problem Statement

When a user selects a different project in the board view's project dropdown, it currently updates the global `currentProject` state, causing the entire application to re-render and switch context (including agent sessions, terminals, and other views). Users expect that selecting a project in the board UI should only affect the board view (Kanban cards/features) and the file explorer panel, allowing them to browse features from different projects without disrupting their active agent sessions or terminal work in another project.

## 2. User Story

As a **developer using DMaker**, I want **project selection in the board view to only update the board and file explorer**, so that **I can browse features from different projects without interrupting my active agent conversation or terminal session in my main project**.

## 3. Acceptance Criteria

### Happy Path
- **GIVEN** a user is on the board view with Project A selected globally
- **WHEN** they select Project B from the board's project dropdown
- **THEN** the Kanban board shows Project B's features, the file explorer shows Project B's files, but the agent view and terminal remain on Project A

### Independent State
- **GIVEN** the board is showing Project B's features while the global context is Project A
- **WHEN** the user switches to the Agent View tab
- **THEN** the agent view still shows Project A's session and working directory

### All Projects Mode
- **GIVEN** a user selects "All Projects" in the board dropdown
- **WHEN** the board loads
- **THEN** features from all projects are displayed in the Kanban board, but the global project context remains unchanged

### Initial Sync
- **GIVEN** a user navigates to the board view for the first time in a session
- **WHEN** the board loads
- **THEN** the board's selected project should initialize to match the global `currentProject`

### Edge Case: No Project Selected Globally
- **GIVEN** no project is selected globally (`currentProject` is null)
- **WHEN** the user views the board
- **THEN** the board can still select and display a project's features without affecting other views

### Error Handling
- **GIVEN** the board is showing Project B's features
- **WHEN** Project B fails to load features (API error, project deleted)
- **THEN** an error message is shown in the board, but the global application state remains unaffected

## 4. Technical Context

| Aspect | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Affected Files | `apps/ui/src/components/views/board-view.tsx`, `apps/ui/src/components/views/board-view/hooks/use-board-features.ts`, `apps/ui/src/components/views/board-view/hooks/use-board-actions.ts`, `apps/ui/src/store/app-store.ts` |
| Dependencies | Zustand, React state |
| Constraints | Must not break existing keyboard shortcuts for global project switching, must preserve "All Projects" mode functionality |
| Patterns to Follow | `use-project-switch-for-sessions.ts` hook pattern for local project selection; file explorer's existing `projectPath` prop pattern |

## 5. Non-Goals

- This feature does NOT change the global project switching behavior in the top navigation bar
- This feature does NOT affect how agent view, terminal view, or settings view handle project selection
- This feature does NOT implement per-project feature caching (only the selected board project's features are loaded at a time)
- This feature does NOT change keyboard shortcuts for global project switching (Cmd+O, Cmd+P)

## 6. Implementation Tasks

```tasks
## Phase 1: Foundation - Create Board-Scoped State

- [ ] T001: Create useBoardProject hook with local project state and sync logic | File: apps/ui/src/components/views/board-view/hooks/use-board-project.ts
- [ ] T002: Add boardSelectedProject and showAllProjectsInBoard to use-board-features hook | File: apps/ui/src/components/views/board-view/hooks/use-board-features.ts
- [ ] T003: Export new hook from hooks index | File: apps/ui/src/components/views/board-view/hooks/index.ts

## Phase 2: Core Implementation - Update Board View

- [ ] T004: Replace global setCurrentProject calls with local board project setter in board-view | File: apps/ui/src/components/views/board-view.tsx
- [ ] T005: Update project dropdown to use board-scoped state instead of global state | File: apps/ui/src/components/views/board-view.tsx
- [ ] T006: Update file explorer prop to use board-scoped project path | File: apps/ui/src/components/views/board-view.tsx
- [ ] T007: Update use-board-features to load features based on board-scoped project | File: apps/ui/src/components/views/board-view/hooks/use-board-features.ts

## Phase 3: Integration - Update Dependent Hooks and Components

- [ ] T008: Update use-board-actions hook to use board-scoped project | File: apps/ui/src/components/views/board-view/hooks/use-board-actions.ts
- [ ] T009: Update use-board-column-features to use board-scoped project | File: apps/ui/src/components/views/board-view/hooks/use-board-column-features.ts
- [ ] T010: Update use-board-drag-drop to use board-scoped project | File: apps/ui/src/components/views/board-view/hooks/use-board-drag-drop.ts
- [ ] T011: Update board-header component to use board-scoped project | File: apps/ui/src/components/views/board-view/board-header.tsx
- [ ] T012: Update KanbanBoard component to receive board-scoped project as prop | File: apps/ui/src/components/views/board-view/kanban-board.tsx

## Phase 4: Cleanup and Verification

- [ ] T013: Ensure dialogs (add/edit feature) use board-scoped project for operations | File: apps/ui/src/components/views/board-view/dialogs/add-feature-dialog.tsx
- [ ] T014: Update edit-feature-dialog to use board-scoped project | File: apps/ui/src/components/views/board-view/dialogs/edit-feature-dialog.tsx
- [ ] T015: Verify agent-chat-panel still uses global currentProject for agent operations | File: apps/ui/src/components/views/board-view/components/agent-chat-panel.tsx
```

## 7. Success Metrics

- [ ] Selecting a project in board dropdown does NOT trigger `setCurrentProject` in global store
- [ ] Board features load based on board-selected project, not global `currentProject`
- [ ] File explorer shows files from board-selected project
- [ ] Agent chat panel in board view continues to use global `currentProject` for agent sessions
- [ ] "All Projects" mode in board works without affecting global project state
- [ ] Initial board load syncs to global `currentProject`
- [ ] No TypeScript errors after changes
- [ ] Existing E2E tests pass

## 8. Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| Agent chat panel in board may accidentally use wrong project | Explicitly pass global `currentProject` to agent chat panel, not board-selected project |
| Feature actions (run, edit, delete) may target wrong project | Ensure all feature mutations use `feature.projectPath` property rather than any global/local project state |
| User confusion about which project is "active" | Consider adding visual indicator in board header when board project differs from global project |
| State synchronization bugs | Initialize board project from global on mount, but do not auto-sync afterward |

---
